preamble: The debuccalization of the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] into the glottal fricative [h] is a common form of weakening, particularly prominent in Brazilian Portuguese and various French-based creoles.

examples.note: Widespread shift where the rhotic is realized as a glottal or back fricative in most dialects.
The 'guttural R' inherited from French is frequently realized as a soft glottal fricative.
Systemic weakening of back dorsal fricatives into the glottal fricative (e.g., PIE *k > PGmc *x > English h).
